data.title: Completion verifier re-checks already-closed DDs at epic level using stricter interpretation than story verifier
data.description: When story 536e-3683 was closed at 7/8 DDs (DD6: 5-SC integration test deferred as architecturally incompatible with behavioral testing standard Rule 5), the epic completion verifier still flagged the corresponding SC7 as FAIL using the same criterion. The story verifier accepted the structural boundary test rationale; the epic verifier did not. This caused an extra unplanned remediation task (adding test_gap_detection_routes_by_child_type) and an additional review/commit cycle. Fix: when all child stories are closed with the verifier's PASS verdict, the epic verifier should trust those story-level verdicts rather than re-verifying each DD from scratch. Alternatively, the epic verifier prompt should note that story-level verifier PASSes are authoritative for their DDs.
